name: Arasaka
tagline: Corporate security, global influence, absolute control.

general.publicSummary: The top global private security contractor in 2020. Founded in 1915 by Saburo Arasaka, Arasaka Corporation is active in 143 countries and declares a global gross revenue of $230B.
general.privateSummary: Arasaka operates as a vertically integrated global power structure combining private military force, corporate security, intelligence gathering, financial leverage and covert operations. It maintains a standing paramilitary force equivalent to a small nation-state, with embedded influence in governments, corporations and financial systems. Its long-term objective is the restoration of Japanese global dominance through economic, military and informational control.

keyMissions.title: Event Security
keyMissions.description: Planning, deployment and execution of security for high-profile events including corporate summits, political gatherings and high-risk public appearances.

keyMissions.title: Area Denial
keyMissions.description: Permanent or temporary control of contested zones through force projection, surveillance and infrastructure lockdown.

keyMissions.title: Private Security
keyMissions.description: Protection of individuals, assets, convoys and infrastructure through layered defensive strategies.

keyMissions.title: Net Watching
keyMissions.description: Offensive and defensive cyber operations including ICE deployment, intrusion, surveillance and data extraction.

keyMissions.title: Corporate Security
keyMissions.description: Full-spectrum corporate defense including physical, digital and political protection.

keyMissions.title: Paramilitary Operations
keyMissions.description: Deployment of heavily armed units for direct combat, suppression or destabilization.

keyMissions.title: Intelligence Gathering
keyMissions.description: Global surveillance network using embedded agents, netrunning and corporate contracts.

keyMissions.title: Covert Operations
keyMissions.description: Black operations including sabotage, assassination, destabilization and regime manipulation.

nodes.label: Saburo Arasaka
nodes.title: CEO
nodes.notes: Ultimate authority over Arasaka corporate doctrine and global strategy.

characterSheet.name: Saburo Arasaka
characterSheet.notes: Confined to powered wheelchair. Personality to be downloaded to AI at death — equipment stands ready 24h at Tokyo HQ infirmary. Cannot survive further cybernetic or organic replacement.

characterSheet.name: Kei Arasaka
characterSheet.notes: CEO of Arasaka in all but name. Travels the world and near orbit constantly under heavy escort. Cold, efficient, detached. Saburo's true successor. Unmarried. Will continue his father's plan.

nodes.label: Hanako Arasaka
nodes.title: Head of Security
nodes.notes: Controls internal and external security forces.

characterSheet.name: Hanako Arasaka
characterSheet.notes: Sheltered youngest daughter of Saburo, raised in the family compound. Only leaves compound ~once/month under heavy escort. Explores the world through the Net instead. Beginning to suspect the dark truth about Arasaka. Secretly communicates with Yorinobu monthly via Net. Saburo intends to arrange her marriage as a corporate alliance. Shintaro is her most trusted protector.

nodes.label: Yorinobu Arasaka
nodes.title: Executive
nodes.notes: Handles corporate relations and dissident factions.

characterSheet.name: Yorinobu Arasaka
characterSheet.notes: Saburo's youngest son, secretly appalled by the corporation's true nature. Fled the compound after graduation. Now leads the Steel Dragons (Kotetsu no Ryu) — Tokyo nomads sworn to expose and destroy Arasaka. Prowls the city at night harassing Arasaka men. Saburo acknowledges he must be destroyed; Kei has sworn to kill him; Hanako still loves him. A Rockerboy in the charismatic rabble-rouser sense, not a musician.

npcSheet.notes: Black-uniformed Arasaka guard deployed at corporate facilities, client sites and public-facing installations. Trained in armed and unarmed urban combat, security procedures and close-quarters fighting. Standard equipment: WSA pistol, Minami 10 or WAA rifle, armor vest, radio, Trauma Team card.

nodes.label: Arasaka Army Soldier
nodes.mission: Deploy in combat and enforce control.

npcSheet.notes: Full-time soldier in Arasaka's corporate army, promoted from the guard corps. Deployed in paramilitary operations, extractions and combat zones worldwide. Wears extended combat armor. Has wider access to heavy weapons, vehicles and specialized equipment.

Experienced non-commissioned officer leading small squads in field operations. Responsible for execution of tactical orders and welfare of assigned troops. Wears full soldier armor with rank chevrons on the left arm.

Veteran former soldier selected for the Arasaka special forces pool. Operates individually or in small teams on high-risk tactical missions. Authorised for custom cyberware, non-traceable weapons and any equipment needed for mission success.

Arasaka covert operative conducting black operations including extractions, assassinations and corporate espionage. Deployed unmarked or attributed to a cover identity. Usually a distinguished former soldier with additional social and infiltration training. Equipment is never traceable to Arasaka.

Intelligence coordinator managing networks of agents and covert assets. Operates from secure facilities, receives mission parameters from the Intelligence Chief and passes actionable tasking to agents in the field. Combines executive authority with operational tradecraft.

Corporate netrunner manning Arasaka's cyber defense operations. Monitors network perimeter, deploys ICE, traces intruders and participates in coordinated cyber attacks. Works from air-gapped neural interface pods in the Netrunning Center.

Senior netrunner leading attack or defense teams. Coordinates multiple netrunners, designs ICE configurations, authorizes offensive operations and writes proprietary Arasaka NET programs. Has access to the most sensitive NET assets.

The men and women who fill Arasaka towers and with whom players are most likely to interact. Conservative dress, understated but expensive. Silver Arasaka logo pin on left lapel. Company Trauma Team card always on person. Packs an Arasaka WSA.

Specialist in economic warfare, market manipulation and investment intelligence. Works in Arasaka Bank and Finance departments. Uses market data and private intelligence to guide corporate investment and destabilization campaigns.

Head of Arasaka Factories engineering division. Responsible for all production engineering, quality standards and process innovation across the Yokohama complex. Reports to the Global Head of Products. Cleared for most manufacturing R&D.

Senior netrunner leading attack or defense teams. Coordinates multiple netrunners, designs ICE configurations, authorizes offensive operations and writes proprietary Arasaka NET programs. Has access to the most sensitive NET assets.

Corporate netrunner manning Arasaka's cyber defense operations. Monitors network perimeter, deploys ICE, traces intruders and participates in coordinated cyber attacks. Works from air-gapped neural interface pods in the Netrunning Center.
Hybrid operative trained in both NET intrusion and field combat. Embedded in Special Forces teams for tactical NET support, on-site ICE bypass and enemy system exploitation during black operations.

Intelligence-division operative combining NET access with field infiltration. Plants surveillance hardware, taps corporate networks from inside client premises and exfiltrates data without physical extraction. Crosses between cyberspace and real-world espionage fluidly.

Commissioned officer commanding platoon-level units in Arasaka's corporate army. Combines tactical field command with corporate loyalty indoctrination. Authorized to call in air assets and heavy support.

Senior military commander overseeing regiment-level operations. A veteran of multiple corporate combat engagements. Serves as a direct bridge between field command and corporate executive direction. Selectively cybered for field longevity.
Commands an Arasaka air squadron. Coordinates air assets with ground forces and directs air support operations. Experienced combat pilot with additional leadership and tactical training.

Commanding officer of the Arasaka Air Division. Oversees all air assets, training, deployment and liaison with the Arasaka Army and Special Forces. Veteran pilot with extensive corporate combat experience.

Military pilot flying AV-4s, V-series Ospreys and helicopters for Arasaka troop transport and air support missions. Combat capable in the cockpit. Trained at Hokkaido and the Narita Airport Security School.

Non-commissioned officer in Arasaka's Air Division. Manages ground crew, pre-flight security checks and passenger security aboard Arasaka aircraft. Combat trained; can serve as door gunner or rapid-deployment trooper.

Experienced guard who has earned promotion to on-site security command. Supervises guard teams, coordinates patrol rotations, liaises with clients. Carries full guard loadout plus leadership authority.

Manages commercial security contracts and client relationships for Arasaka Security or Arasaka Products. Oversees contract negotiation, client onboarding and service delivery tracking. Personable, well-dressed, quietly threatening.

The men and women who fill Arasaka towers and with whom players are most likely to interact. Conservative dress, understated but expensive. Silver Arasaka logo pin on left lapel. Company Trauma Team card always on person. Packs an Arasaka WSA.
Weapons and security system engineer assigned to Arasaka R&D Tech Labs. Designs and prototypes new hardware. Not cleared for the northern Honshu R&D facility. Works in a major tower R&D department.

Leads a Tech Lab team within Arasaka R&D. Manages project timelines, mentors engineers, liaises with corporate product divisions and ensures projects meet Arasaka performance standards. Cleared for some sensitive R&D data.

Leads the Net Lab research team within R&D. Oversees development of proprietary Arasaka ICE, attack programs and NET architecture. Reports directly to the Research Chief and interacts with the Honshu database team.

NET researcher assigned to Arasaka R&D labs. Develops new ICE, tests exploits against captured systems and builds proprietary programs. Operates in the most classified NET environments including the northern Honshu database subgrid.

Senior scientist overseeing an R&D division. Manages multi-year classified projects and coordinates between Tech Labs, Net Labs and the Honshu R&D facility. Cleared at the highest R&D security level short of the inner circle.

Manages commercial security contracts and client relationships for Arasaka Security or Arasaka Products. Oversees contract negotiation, client onboarding and service delivery tracking. Personable, well-dressed, quietly threatening.

The men and women who fill Arasaka towers and with whom players are most likely to interact. Conservative dress, understated but expensive. Silver Arasaka logo pin on left lapel. Company Trauma Team card always on person. Packs an Arasaka WSA.

Manages one of Arasaka's Yokohama production facilities. Oversees assembly lines, worker safety, output quotas and supply chain coordination. Conservative corporate dress; carries a WSA per company policy.
Weapons and security system engineer assigned to Arasaka R&D Tech Labs. Designs and prototypes new hardware. Not cleared for the northern Honshu R&D facility. Works in a major tower R&D department.

Assembly-line worker at the Yokohama complex. Wears white uniform with shoulder logo. Allowed to carry a sidearm per company policy and receives rudimentary security training. Defers to executives and bows in Japanese offices.

Commands a Special Forces team. Former elite operative elevated to command. Coordinates multi-asset operations and serves as tactical intelligence link to handler network. Trusted with knowledge of black operation targets.

groups.name: Security
groups.notes: Core armed division handling protection, enforcement and warfare.

groups.name: Logistics
groups.notes: Transport, supply chain and convoy operations.

locations.name: Arasaka Tower
locations.description: Massive corporate arcology integrating executive command, military barracks, netrunning floors, detention centers and armored infrastructure.

locations.name: Arasaka Tower
locations.description: Twin-spire corporate tower splitting a quarter of the way up, joined by enclosed skyways. Landing pads for AV-series. Landscaped plaza at base, subterranean vehicle and barracks levels.

locations.name: Arasaka Tower
locations.description: Major tower in the financial district. Houses the East Coast Security and Banking operations. Among the tallest buildings in the city.

locations.name: Arasaka Tower
locations.description: West Coast operations hub. Heavy entertainment industry bodyguard contracts and media security. Full paramilitary complement.

locations.name: Arasaka Tower
locations.description: European Security and Banking hub. Manages EU government police contracts including Belfast and several Eastern European cities.

locations.name: Arasaka Tower
locations.description: European commercial security sales and political liaison office. Manages French and Southern European accounts.

locations.name: Arasaka Tower
locations.description: Financial and diplomatic hub. Houses high-value banking operations and discreet political liaison. ESA Space Operations School coordination.

locations.name: Arasaka Tower
locations.description: Northern European security and manufacturing hub. Manages German and Nordic accounts.

locations.name: Arasaka Tower
locations.description: Asia-Pacific operations hub outside Japan. Gateway for Southeast Asian security contracts and banking. Major intelligence collection node.

locations.name: Arasaka Tower
locations.description: Korean peninsula operations office. Police and corporate security contracts in Seoul metropolitan area.

locations.name: Arasaka Tower
locations.description: Southeast Asia operations hub. Manages police contracts in Singapore, manages regional banking operations.

locations.name: Arasaka Tower
locations.description: Oceania regional hub. Manages Australian corporate security contracts and police services.

locations.name: Arasaka Tower
locations.description: Canadian operations hub. Close to US East Coast operations. French-language capabilities available.

locations.name: Arasaka Tower
locations.description: Midwest US security and police contracting hub. Several major corporate accounts in the Chicago metro.

locations.name: Arasaka Office
locations.description: Pacific crossroads office. Manages Hawaii police contract and serves as Pacific relay for Tokyo-US communications.

locations.name: Arasaka Tower
locations.description: African operations hub. Manages security contracts across sub-Saharan Africa. Growing market.

locations.name: Arasaka Tower
locations.description: South American hub. Houses the Jungle Operations School nearby in the Brazilian interior. Manages regional security contracts.

locations.name: Arasaka Tower
locations.description: Iberian and North African operations hub.

locations.name: Arasaka Tower
locations.description: Italian and Mediterranean operations office. Political liaison center for Vatican and EU institutions.

locations.name: Arasaka Tower
locations.description: Nordic operations hub. Winter Operations Camp nearby in the Rocky Mountains of northern Sweden. Manages Scandinavian accounts.

locations.name: Arasaka Tower
locations.description: Southeast Asia secondary hub. Manages Thai and Indochina accounts.

typicalOffices.name: Security Quarters
typicalOffices.description: Barracks, armories, deployment zones, tactical briefing rooms, prisoner holding areas and rapid response hangars.
typicalOffices.security: Full surveillance, biometric locks, automated turrets, controlled access

typicalOffices.name: Training Grounds
typicalOffices.description: Live-fire ranges, urban combat simulators, VR tactical environments and physical conditioning facilities.

typicalOffices.name: Netrunning Center
typicalOffices.description: Air-gapped cyber warfare hub with ICE arrays, neural interface pods and data vaults.

typicalOffices.name: Intelligence Hub
typicalOffices.description: Data aggregation, surveillance monitoring and covert operations planning center.

typicalOffices.name: Weapons Factory
typicalOffices.description: Production lines for firearms, ammunition, cyberware, drones and military vehicles.

typicalOffices.name: Guard Barracks
typicalOffices.description: On-site billets for guards and soldiers quartered at the tower. Includes dormitories, equipment lockers, mess hall and briefing rooms. Between 100–200 guards and 50–100 soldiers are billeted at any major tower at all times.

typicalOffices.name: Armory
typicalOffices.description: Weapons storage and maintenance facility. Holds standard-issue WAA rifles, WMA Minami 10s, WSA pistols, WCAA shotguns and assorted heavy weapons. Also maintains sales-prepped combat vehicles (Kumas, Riot 8s, Combat 10s) in restricted underground bays.

typicalOffices.name: Detention Block
typicalOffices.description: Secured holding area in the subterranean levels of Arasaka towers. Includes individual cells, interrogation rooms and a processing area. Used for holding captured infiltrators, corporate adversaries and individuals of intelligence value.
typicalOffices.security: Maximum physical security, no NET access

typicalOffices.name: Medical Bay
typicalOffices.description: Surgery-capable infirmary staffed by Arasaka medical personnel. Handles trauma treatment, cybernetic installation and the company Trauma Team card network. The Tokyo HQ infirmary holds a dedicated AI personality upload rig on 24h standby for Saburo Arasaka.

typicalOffices.name: Executive Floor
typicalOffices.description: Upper floors of Arasaka towers housing the regional director offices, board conference rooms, executive suites and private penthouses. Decorated in rich synthetic marble with Japanese contemporary art. Luxury increases with floor height.
typicalOffices.security: Express elevator control, biometric locks, plainclothes guards

typicalOffices.name: Banking Floor
typicalOffices.description: Secure banking operations area handling corporate accounts, high-value loans, investment management and financial intelligence. Client suites for exclusive meetings with corporate account holders. All transactions classified — not on any public retrieval system.
typicalOffices.security: Full surveillance, encrypted systems, background-checked staff only

typicalOffices.name: Communications Center
typicalOffices.description: Satellite uplink hub, surveillance monitoring station and internal communications backbone. Staffed 24h by netrunners scanning for intrusion and communications officers coordinating between global Arasaka offices. Also monitors and coordinates special response teams.

items.name: WAA Bullpup Rifle
items.description: Bullpup assault rifle with integral optical sight and smartgun chip. Standard-issue for Arasaka soldiers. Takes 5, 15 or 30 round mags. Reliable in all conditions. Stats: RIF +1 N C 5D6(5.56mm) 30rd ROF30 VR 400m

items.name: WMA Minami 10
items.description: Compact submachine gun standard-issued to Arasaka security troops. Also available silenced for 700eb (ACC-1, concealability L). Stats: SMG 0 J E 2D6+3(10mm) 40rd ROF20 VR 200m

items.name: WSA Automatic Pistol
items.description: Standard sidearm for all Arasaka troops and executives. Reliable, accurate, compact. Stats: P 0 J C 2D6+3(10mm) 15rd ROF2 VR 50m

items.name: WCAA Assault Shotgun
items.description: Close-quarters shotgun favored in maritime and confined-space operations. Also used by police gang squads. Stats: SHT -1 N C 4D6(00) 20rd ROF10 ST 50m

items.name: Arasaka AV-4
items.description: Fast armored aerial vehicle used for rapid deployment and executive transport. Carries up to 4 passengers plus crew. Standard complement at every major Arasaka tower.

items.name: Arasaka Armored Convoy
items.description: Hardened Lexus ZS-5000SR convoy with Arasaka security retrofit. Independent air supply, bulletproof glass, armored body, smoke/gas/oil ejectors, proximity mines. Body SP30 SDP150.

items.name: Corporate Security Contract
items.description: Full-spectrum on-site security forces deployable from 1 to 1,000 agents per contract. 24h service. Options: gender, language (EN/JP/DE/FR/ES/IT/ZH/KR), armament level, visibility, specialization. Backed by Lloyd's of London breach guarantee.

items.name: Event Security Package
items.description: Tiered security for corporate summits, political appearances and high-risk public events. Includes perimeter control, plainclothes guards, counter-sniper teams and rapid response. All-male, all-female or mixed units available.

items.name: Convoy Protection
items.description: Armed escort and secure transport operations for personnel, assets and sensitive cargo. Arasaka retrofitted vehicles, armed outriders, route surveillance and contingency response.

items.name: Net Defense Package
items.description: Proprietary Arasaka ICE systems (white and black) deployed and operated exclusively by Arasaka netrunners. Includes threat monitoring, intrusion countermeasures and forensic breach analysis. ICE code is never disclosed.

items.name: Covert Operations Package
items.description: Black operations including corporate extraction, sabotage, targeted assassination and regime destabilization. Always conducted unmarked or attributed to a third party. Plausible deniability guaranteed. Contact your Arasaka liaison only.

items.name: WXA Computer Aimed Weapon
items.description: Belt-fed computer-aimed and fired machine gun. Programs to key on motion or heat, fire in any arc 10-360° at ranges 2-400m. Remote monitor capable. IFF pulser compatible. Includes 500rd disintegrating belt. Stats: RIF 0 N R 6D6+2(7.62mm) 500rd ROF20 VR 400m

items.name: Barrett-Arasaka Light 20
items.description: Long-range sniping weapon standard to special ops teams. Also used as a borg-stopper by police forces. Heavy 20mm shell reduces armor 2pts per hit. Stats: HVY 0 N R 4D10(20mm) 10rd ROF1 VR 450m

items.name: Sternmeyer M-5A SAW
items.description: Squad automatic weapon purchased from Sternmeyer Waffenfabrik. Bipod-mounted, belt-fed 7.62mm weapon common to Arasaka soldiers and special ops. Vehicle-mountable. Fires same belts as WXA. Stats: HVY -1 N R 6D6+2(7.62mm) 200rd ROF20 ST 400m

items.name: Kendachi Monoknife
items.description: Crystalline blade combat knife favored by Arasaka security troops. Not usable as a tool. Monomolecular edge holds an exceptional cutting surface. Stats: Melee +1 N R 4D6

items.name: Kuma Light Troop Vehicle
items.description: Small four-wheel drive vehicle similar to a jeep. Seats 4. Roll bar with coaxial SAW mount and floodlight fittings. Agile, fast, handles ruined urban terrain. Available to the public. SDP50 SP20. Top speed: 90mph.

items.name: Riot 8 LUAV
items.description: Urban crowd suppression vehicle. Delivers 8 armed troops. Fire-resistant hull SDP200 SP30. Water cannon (30m stream/10m steam), grenade launcher (300m, 180° arc). Satellite uplink, cyber-capable, crew 2. Top speed: 120mph.

items.name: Combat 10 HAV
items.description: Heavy 8-wheel all-terrain assault vehicle. Carries 10 armored troops. 360° turret with minigun and light cannon (8D6, 5m radius, 1000m). Smoke/gas ejectors, coaxial SAW mount. Hull SP50 SDP250. Cyber-capable, satellite uplink. Top speed: 80mph.

items.name: Lexus ZS-5000SR
items.description: Executive security retrofit on Toyota Lexus platform. Coupe and limousine variants. Independent air supply (4hrs), bulletproof windows, armored engine/body/underbody, smoke/gas/oil ejectors, solid tires, 10 proximity mines (6D6). Body SP30 SDP150. Top speed: 140mph.

items.name: Toshiba PRC-10 Radio
items.description: Standard issue encrypted two-way radio for guards and soldiers. Belt pack with headset earpiece/mic. 10-channel, modular cypher chip (5-digit code lock), cyber audio/video linkable. Range ~1 mile. 50eb per cypher chip.

items.name: Personal Bodyguard Service
items.description: Individual or team bodyguard service for celebrities, executives, heads of state and VIPs. Options: gender, dress style, age, visibility, armament, race, nationality. Single glamour-guard option popular in entertainment circles. Life insurance 1M–100M€ per client.

items.name: Security Consultancy
items.description: On-site security audit and recommendation service. Arasaka consultants inspect facilities, analyse threat profiles and prescribe optimal security architecture, force levels and countermeasure installations. Available from personal to worldwide corporate scale.

items.name: Arasaka Security Prospectus Chip
items.description: Knowledge chip listing all Arasaka Security services, options and prices. +3 to Knowledge: Arasaka Security Services. Available in optical disk, braindance or VR form. Contains no information usable for penetrating Arasaka security.

items.name: Corporate Banking Services
items.description: Exclusive corporate and business account management, high-yield investment portfolio and selective lending. The Arasaka Bank does not advertise publicly — services are offered by direct approach only. Handles accounts for select high-net-worth individuals and allied corporations.

publicData.marketingStrategy: Arasaka advertises its Security division through two channels: general visibility (billboards, holo-ads, motionboards, corporate nets) maintaining constant public awareness of the Arasaka logo; and upscale promotion via corporate periodicals like 'C' Magazine targeting corporate decision-makers. Bodyguard services use glossy high-end ads featuring client testimonials. A knowledge chip (100eb, +3 Arasaka Services Knowledge) is available for executives evaluating contracts. The Bank operates exclusively by direct approach — no public advertising. Manufacturing is promoted in police journals, military publications and electronics magazines.
publicData.reputation: Rated #1 in property and proprietary information protection by 'C' Magazine. Security contracts backed by Lloyd's of London breach guarantee. If Arasaka security is breached, the corporation compensates the full assessed value of all compromised materials. Bodyguard clients insured for 1M–100M€.
publicData.corporateValue: 475 billion eurodollars in total assets. Among the two or three corporations approaching the half-trillion mark.

privateData.realRevenue: Classified — substantially higher than declared through subsidiary obfuscation and laundering via dummy corporations and the Arasaka Bank.
privateData.covertBudget: Massive — sufficient to fund a standing paramilitary force, global surveillance network, black operations cell, political bribery at hundreds of government levels and ongoing AI personality preservation infrastructure.
privateData.masterPlan: Saburo's true goal: the return of Japan to imperial glory through economic and military dominance. The Arasaka Corporation is the instrument. Security contracts provide intelligence. The bank provides money-laundering and leverage. Manufacturing provides weapons. Black operations provide control. Hundreds of politicians, executives and law enforcement agents worldwide are owned by Arasaka. Japanese constitutional rearmament clause trashed in 1996 by Arasaka-owned politicians. Saburo is rebuilding Japan's national armed forces.
privateData.knownEnemies: Militech (direct military competitor, ongoing corporate warfare), Infocomp (investigative database), Network News 54 (investigative journalists — several reporters eliminated), Tycho mass driver colony (existential threat — would target Tokyo HQ if Arasaka imperial conquest becomes visible).
privateData.securityNote: The most dangerous aspect of Arasaka is that you cannot hide your secrets from the people you hired to protect them. Every security contract feeds the northern Honshu database.

openings.title: Security Guard
openings.description: Protect Arasaka-contracted facilities, personnel and assets. Guards wear the black Arasaka uniform (boots, gloves, jumpsuit, armor vest, cap). Equipped with WSA pistol and WMA Minami 10. Expected to repulse corporate assaults, extractions and sabotage attempts with quick response capability. On-site billetted or placed with client. 24h service.
openings.requirements: Physical fitness. Combat-capable. Weapons familiarity. Guards may earn cybernetic enhancement from Arasaka through meritorious service. Conversational English required.
openings.benefits: Arasaka Trauma Team card. Uniform and equipment provided. Cybernetic enhancement available for distinguished service. Promotion to soldier possible.

openings.title: Security Soldier
openings.description: Full-time soldier in Arasaka's corporate army, promoted exclusively from the guard corps. Deployed in paramilitary operations abroad (current theaters include Guinea, Panama, Iraq and others). Wears extended combat armor (visor helmet SP20, torso+arm plate SP18, combat trousers SP4). Access to heavy weapons, vehicles and specialized equipment including the AV-4 and V-24B Osprey combat variant.
openings.requirements: Three or more years of military service or equivalent combat experience. Advanced weapons proficiency. Physical condition above average. Must have served as Arasaka Security Guard. Loyalty seminar completion mandatory.
openings.benefits: Arasaka Trauma Team card. Extended combat armor issued. Access to corporate cyberware program. Potential selection for Special Forces or Bodyguard programs.

openings.title: Netrunner
openings.description: Staff the Arasaka cyber warfare divisions defending corporate and client networks. Deploy, monitor and maintain proprietary Arasaka ICE. Trace intruders, conduct offensive NET operations and participate in coordinated black NET assaults under Lead Netrunner direction. Work from air-gapped neural interface pods in secured Netrunning Centers.
openings.requirements: Advanced NET infrastructure knowledge. Cyberdeck proficiency. Programming and System Knowledge skills. Interface implants required — Arasaka will cover installation cost for qualified candidates. Security clearance investigation mandatory.
openings.benefits: Arasaka Trauma Team card. Cyberdeck provided. Interface implant cost covered. Access to Arasaka proprietary NET program library.

openings.title: Special Agent
openings.description: Conduct covert operations defending Arasaka interests and those of its clients. Missions include extractions, targeted terminations, corporate espionage, blackmail operations and counter-infiltration. Always operate unmarked — equipment and identity never traceable to Arasaka. Selected exclusively from former soldiers who have distinguished themselves. Receive specialized retraining in social infiltration, tradecraft and covert weapons.
openings.requirements: Distinguished Arasaka military service record. Exceptional physical and mental aptitude. Fast reaction times. Broad general knowledge base. Recommended by a serving senior agent or Intelligence Chief. Total loyalty to the Arasaka Corporation.
openings.benefits: Classified. Custom cyberware. Operational budget. Full identity support infrastructure.
